00:21:07	Jason Parker:	I think the important piece here is that the principle is universal.  The example is regionally, culturally specific.  But I think that is up to you as the professional to bring the application that is unique.  That is what will separate you out.

00:43:34	Vladimir Bushin:	In @Ed Fegan Have you heard of Einstein? For those who didn't hear of Albert Einstain was a god father of the theory of relativity. How many of you hear of Dr. Robert Cialdini?
00:43:46	Eily Cartwright:	Reacted to "In @Ed Fegan Have yo..." with ❤️
00:43:46	Vladimir Bushin:	Dr. Dialdini is the godfather of Influence
00:43:47	stefano:	Thanks Bas, thanks Eily
00:48:06	Jason Parker:	I usually introduce Social Influence as the "Science of Yes.  How can we get more Yes' than Nos."  I then introduce Dr. Cialdini as the most cited author in the world on this topic.  Whether they have heard of him or not, IMHO, it establishes what I am about to teach is credible.  You don't become the most cited author in the world on a topic without having authority.  In this case, not because I have said so but because the majority of his peers (social proof) have said so.
00:49:24	Jason Parker:	@Brian Ahearn Dr. Jermone Groopman wrote a book called "How Doctors think".  He suggests something very similar to your example.  In a general sense, is just to simply ask, "Aside what you are considering here, what else could it possible be?"
